body_txt: Two characters live in one house or apartment. Unfortunately, they don't like each other, or just happen to be angry with each other at the moment (especially in a Feud Episode ). Eventually one of them marks a dividing line (with paint, colored chalk, or long strip of tape) in the middle of their shared residence, explaining: "This is my side; this is yours. Don't go on my part!" Naturally, it's Serious Business . Eventually complications will begin to show up, like how long the line is expected to be maintained, guests being forced to follow those rules, or necessities like the fridge or bathroom being available only on one side. Subtrope of Roommate Drama . Sometimes, the "Getting My Own Room" Plot is the solution to their bickering. Other times the characters resolve their conflict, learn An Aesop about sharing, and remove the line. Related to This Is My Chair , which is usually a throwaway gag. The Property Line is the same idea on a larger scale. The Chaste Separating Sword is an expression of this dividing a bed. See also the Judgment of Solomon . Truth in Television , as those who have ever had an argument with a sibling, roommate, or Soviet Bloc can attest to.
